content.text: I'll help you see your Docker stacks on Mittwald. Let me start by listing your projects and then we can look at the stacks within them.

input.description: List Mittwald projects and Docker stacks
input.prompt:
The user wants to see their Docker stacks on Mittwald. Use these MCP tools to help:

1. First, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_project_list_projects to list all available projects
2. Then for each project, call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_container_list_container_recipes to get the Docker stacks/containers available
3. Also call mcp__mittwald__mittwald_container_list_containers to see deployed containers

Return a comprehensive summary showing:
- All projects with their IDs
- Available container/Docker stacks in each project
- Any deployed containers

Format the output clearly showing the project structure and available stacks.

content.content:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

Manage containers

USAGE
  $ mw container COMMAND

COMMANDS
  container cp            Copy files/folders between a container and the local
                          filesystem
  container delete        Delete a container
  container exec          Execute a command in a container via SSH
                          non-interactively.
  container list          List containers belonging to a project.
  container logs          Display logs of a specific container.
  container ls            List containers belonging to a project.
  container port-forward  Forward a container port to a local port
  container recreate      Recreates a container.
  container restart       Restarts a container.
  container rm            Delete a container
  container run           Creates and starts a new container.
  container ssh           Connect to a container via SSH
  container start         Starts a stopped container.
  container stop          Stops a running container.
  container update        Updates an existing container.
